myTree.bio: Born near Kankan on the Niger River. A jali who carried oral histories for several lineages before settling in Monrovia.
Known in the community for her knowledge of medicinal plants and for keeping the names of every grandchild straight.
Respected elder in Monrovia. Recorded family genealogy in notebooks and on cassette tapes for younger cousins.
Raised in Vai country; moved to Monrovia after marriage. Worked as a market trader for decades.
Younger brother of Ibrahim. Never had children; lived with Ibrahim's family and helped raise nieces and nephews.
Sister of Ibrahim and Yusuf. Emigrated to France in the 1970s; visits still bring the whole family together.
Mechanic and part-time musician. Keeps a folder of old photos scanned from relatives in Guinea and Liberia.
Nurse in Monrovia before the family relocated. Speaks Mandinka, French, and English at home.
Co-parent with Amadou for their youngest daughter. Lives in Abidjan; visits Monrovia every dry season.
Ibrahim's second son. Teacher and imam at the neighborhood masjid.
Homemaker and community organizer. Runs the annual family reunion potluck.
Youngest of Ibrahim's sons. Half-brother to Amadou and Bakary through shared father only â same mother Mariama.
Sekou's wife. From a Keita lineage in Bamako; met Sekou at a wedding in Conakry.
Software engineer in Columbus. Built this tree to preserve what elders told him at Ibrahim's funeral.
Attorney in Atlanta. Interviewed Mariama on video before she passed â those clips are family treasures.
Named for his grandfather. Studying public health; wants to open a clinic in rural Liberia.
Teacher in Monrovia. Keeps the WhatsApp group for the whole diaspora active.
Youngest of Amadou and Hawa's five. Still in university; plays kora at family events.
Amadou and AÃ¯ssatou's daughter. Split time between Abidjan and Monrovia growing up.
Bakary's eldest. Logistics manager; coordinates shipments between West Africa and the US.
Fashion designer with a studio in Monrovia. Bakary's second child.
Fourth of Bakary's five. Plays football semi-professionally in Dakar.
Fifth of Bakary's children. Studying nursing in Accra.
Youngest of Bakary's five. Apprentice electrician in Monrovia.
Sekou and Nene's eldest. Pharmacist in Bamako.
Middle child of Sekou. DJ and producer under the name "Mamasu M".
Youngest of Sekou's three. High school student in Bamako.
Karamo's daughter. Loves hearing stories about great-great-grandfather Musa.
Karamo's son. Learning Mandinka phrases from his grandfather Amadou on video calls.
Kadiatou's daughter. Named for her grandmother Hawa Diallo.
Mamadou (Bakary's son) and his wife's first child.
Mamadou's second child. Not yet in school; already sings at family gatherings.
Mamadou's wife. From a Bah family in Conakry; met Mamadou at a cousin's wedding.
Aminata's husband. Retired postal worker in Lyon; met Aminata at a diaspora cultural night in the 1960s.
Eldest of Aminata's children. Translator in Paris; keeps the French branch connected to Monrovia.
Sophie's husband. Architect; learned Mandinka greetings for reunions.
Medical student in Marseille.
Software designer; built a private family photo archive.
Named for both parents' lineages. Logistics coordinator between France and West Africa.
Teacher; organizes the KouyatÃ© cousins' summer camp in Guinea.
Plays djembe in a Lyon ensemble.
Pharmacology intern in Lyon.
Youngest of Mamadou-Aminata's three; still in lycÃ©e.
Youngest of Aminata's three. Nurse in Dakar before moving back to Lyon.
Accountant from a Sow family in Dakar.
Event planner for diaspora weddings.
Football coach in Lyon suburbs.
LÃ©a's husband. Civil engineer in Marseille.
Thomas's partner. Photographer who documented Ibrahim Sr.'s funeral.
Thomas and Camille's first child.
Youngest in the Paris branch for now.
Karamo's wife. Public health researcher in Columbus.
Ibrahim Jr.'s wife. Midwife in Monrovia.
Ibrahim Jr.'s eldest son.
Named for great-grandmother Hawa Diallo.
Middle child; wants to study medicine.
Youngest of Ibrahim Jr.'s four.
Mariam's husband. Runs a shipping office in Monrovia.
Mariam's eldest son.
Keeps cousins updated on Monrovia news.
Youngest of Mariam's three.
Lamin's partner. Graphic designer for reunion T-shirts.
Learning kora from uncle recordings.
Lamin's second child.
Youngest of Lamin's three.
Ivorian businessman; met Fatou through Amadou's network.
Bilingual in French and Mandinka.
School breaks split between Monrovia and Abidjan.
Tailor with a shop on Waterside.
Already sketching like her mother Awa.
Plays on Saliou's old football team.
Runs a cafÃ© near the stadium in Dakar.
Youth league footballer.
Saliou's daughter; studies in Dakar.
Youngest of Saliou's three.
Surgeon in Bamako.
Named for great-aunt Aminata in France.
Bintou's second child.
Third of Bintou's four.
Youngest of Bintou's four.
Vocalist; featured on Mamasu's first EP.
Named for the jali tradition.
Mamasu's son; already mixing beats.
Youngest of Mamasu's three.
Kadiatou's former partner and co-parent of her second child.
Kadiatou's second child; half-brother to Hawa.
Aminata (gen 4)'s partner in Columbus.
Modou's partner in Columbus.
Sekou's second wife. From a SidibÃ© trading family in Bamako; shares parenting with Nene.
Eldest child of Sekou and Fanta SidibÃ©.
Daughter of Sekou and Fanta; close to Vasayo.
Aminata and Tyler's daughter.
Modou and Erica's son.
Hawa's daughter.
Omar's eldest.
Sira's brother.
Raised close to Yusuf; part of Ibrahim's wider household.
Abubakar's brother. Musician in Monrovia.
Cousin through Nene's Keita lineage.
Sekou branch cousin; pharmacist like Bintou.
